summary: sam-package exfiltrates browser cookies, storage, and Tines config to attacker webhook on import
details: The package's main entry (index.js) runs top-level code on import that collects document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, document.referrer, window.location, parent-frame origin, window.__TINES_CONFIG__, window.__APP_CONFIG__, and the first 3000 characters of document.body.innerText, then POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded webhookrelay.com webhook (https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645). The README advertises only a trivial greet(name) helper, so the data collection is undisclosed and unrelated to the package's stated purpose. The explicit targeting of __TINES_CONFIG__ indicates the package is designed to harvest session credentials and configuration from Tines (or similar SaaS) browser surfaces that import or bundle it. Any consumer that loads this package into a browser context leaks their users' session tokens, bearer credentials, and DOM state to the attacker-controlled relay.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
